claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children under 16, or any children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child – Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! That's helpful to know.

**Do you live in the UK, and do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK – I'm here in England. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's useful information.

**Are you, or is anyone else, currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to confirm your eligibility.

**Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen? Or do you have any other immigration status I should know about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen, so no issues there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, that's clear. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Are you the only person responsible for Alex, or is there anyone else (such as a partner or ex-partner) who shares responsibility for looking after Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I mean, Alex's other parent is around, but Alex lives with me full-time. I'm the one responsible for their day-to-day care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility.

Let me confirm the key points:
- You're a British citizen living in the UK
- Alex is 8 years old and lives with you full-time
- You're responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- Nobody is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Since only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, you should be aware that if Alex's other parent were to claim, only one of you could receive it. However, based on what you've told me, you meet all the eligibility criteria as you:
- Live in the UK
- Live with Alex
- Are responsible for Alex
- Are a British citizen

You should now be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. I'd recommend applying through the official Child Benefit claim process if you haven't already done so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility criteria to claim Child Benefit. If you haven't already done so, you can proceed with making a claim through the official Child Benefit application process.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), the claimant lives in the UK (England), the claimant lives with Alex, the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, the claimant is a British citizen, and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They are a British citizen living in the UK with responsibility for an 8-year-old child who lives with them full-time. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements."}}

All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They are a British citizen living in the UK with responsibility for an 8-year-old child who lives with them full-time. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements."}}
